722	\begin{lemma}[Relative tensor of module presheaves as a coequalizer]
723	  \label{lem:relativeTensor_as_coequalizer}
724	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.relativeTensorCoequalizerIso}
725	  \uses{lem:presheafModule_monoidal_mathlib,
726	    lem:relativeTensor_objectwise_coequalizer,
727	    lem:evaluationJointlyReflectsColimits_mathlib,
728	    lem:presheaf_tensorObj_obj_mathlib,
729	    def:relTensorActL, def:relTensorActR, def:relTensorProj}
730	  Let \(\mathcal{P}, \mathcal{Q}\) be presheaves of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules. The
731	  underlying abelian-group presheaf of their relative tensor product
732	  \(\mathcal{P} \otimes_{p,\mathcal{O}_X} \mathcal{Q}\)
733	  (\cref{lem:presheafModule_monoidal_mathlib}) is naturally the coequalizer of the
734	  parallel pair
735	  \[
736	    \mathcal{P} \otimes_{\mathbb{Z}} \mathcal{O}_X \otimes_{\mathbb{Z}} \mathcal{Q}
737	    \;\rightrightarrows\;
738	    \mathcal{P} \otimes_{\mathbb{Z}} \mathcal{Q}
739	  \]
740	  of abelian-group presheaves, where the first map acts the ring factor on the left,
741	  \(p \otimes r \otimes q \mapsto (r \cdot p) \otimes q\), and the second on the
742	  right, \(p \otimes r \otimes q \mapsto p \otimes (r \cdot q)\). The identification
743	  is objectwise and natural in \(U\).
744	\end{lemma}
745	
746	\begin{proof}
747	  \uses{lem:presheafModule_monoidal_mathlib,
748	    lem:relativeTensor_objectwise_coequalizer,
749	    lem:evaluationJointlyReflectsColimits_mathlib,
750	    lem:presheaf_tensorObj_obj_mathlib,
751	    def:relTensorActL, def:relTensorActR, def:relTensorProj}
752	  The argument promotes the objectwise coequalizer of
753	  \cref{lem:relativeTensor_objectwise_coequalizer} to the functor category, in
754	  three steps.
755	
756	  \textbf{1. Objectwise.} For each open \(U\), apply
757	  \cref{lem:relativeTensor_objectwise_coequalizer} with \(S = \mathcal{O}_X(U)\),
758	  \(M = \mathcal{P}(U)\), \(N = \mathcal{Q}(U)\): the abelian group
759	  \(\mathcal{P}(U) \otimes_{\mathcal{O}_X(U)} \mathcal{Q}(U)\) is the coequalizer
760	  in \(\mathrm{AddCommGrp}\) of the two \(\mathcal{O}_X(U)\)-action maps
761	  \[
762	    \mathcal{P}(U) \otimes_{\mathbb{Z}} \mathcal{O}_X(U)
763	      \otimes_{\mathbb{Z}} \mathcal{Q}(U)
764	    \;\rightrightarrows\;
765	    \mathcal{P}(U) \otimes_{\mathbb{Z}} \mathcal{Q}(U),
766	  \]
767	  with cofork map the projection \(\pi_U\).
768	
769	  \textbf{2. Naturality / promotion.} The three objectwise families are precisely
770	  the natural transformations of presheaves on \(X\)
771	  \[
772	    \mathcal{P} \otimes_{\mathbb{Z}} \mathcal{O}_X
773	      \otimes_{\mathbb{Z}} \mathcal{Q}
774	    \;\overset{a_L,\,a_R}{\rightrightarrows}\;
775	    \mathcal{P} \otimes_{\mathbb{Z}} \mathcal{Q}
776	    \;\overset{\pi}{\longrightarrow}\;
777	    \bigl(U \mapsto \mathcal{P}(U) \otimes_{\mathcal{O}_X(U)} \mathcal{Q}(U)\bigr)
778	  \]
779	  built in the previous definitions: the parallel pair is the left- and
780	  right-action transformations \(a_L\) (\cref{def:relTensorActL}) and \(a_R\)
781	  (\cref{def:relTensorActR}) from the triple presheaf
782	  (\cref{def:relTensorTriplePresheaf}) to the \(\mathbb{Z}\)-tensor presheaf
783	  (\cref{def:relTensorDomainPresheaf}), and the cofork leg is the projection
784	  \(\pi\) (\cref{def:relTensorProj}) onto the apex. These are morphisms of
785	  functors \((\mathrm{Opens}\,X)^{\mathrm{op}} \to \mathrm{Ab}\); naturality of
786	  each in \(U\) is the compatibility of the module action with the restriction
787	  maps of \(\mathcal{P}, \mathcal{O}_X, \mathcal{Q}\) (the restriction of
788	  \(s \cdot m\) is \((s|_V) \cdot (m|_V)\), and likewise for the projection),
789	  recorded in those definitions. Objectwise at each \(U\) the cofork
790	  \((a_L, a_R, \pi)_U\) is the colimit cofork of step 1. Colimits in a functor
791	  category are computed objectwise --- by
792	  \cref{lem:evaluationJointlyReflectsColimits_mathlib} a cocone is a colimit as
793	  soon as every evaluation \((\mathrm{ev}_U)\) of it is --- so the objectwise
794	  coequalizers of step 1 promote to a coequalizer of the displayed parallel pair
795	  \(a_L, a_R\) with cofork \(\pi\) in
796	  \((\mathrm{Opens}\,X)^{\mathrm{op}} \to \mathrm{Ab}\).
797	
798	  \textbf{3. Identification of the apex.} The apex presheaf
799	  \(U \mapsto \mathcal{P}(U) \otimes_{\mathcal{O}_X(U)} \mathcal{Q}(U)\) is, by the
800	  objectwise tensor formula of the presheaf monoidal structure
801	  (\cref{lem:presheaf_tensorObj_obj_mathlib}: \((\mathcal{M}_1 \otimes_p
802	  \mathcal{M}_2)(U) = \mathcal{M}_1(U) \otimes_{\mathcal{O}_X(U)}
803	  \mathcal{M}_2(U)\)), exactly the underlying abelian-group presheaf of the
804	  relative tensor product \(\mathcal{P} \otimes_{p,\mathcal{O}_X} \mathcal{Q}\)
805	  (\cref{lem:presheafModule_monoidal_mathlib}). Transporting the colimit of step 2
806	  along this identification exhibits
807	  \(\mathcal{P} \otimes_{p,\mathcal{O}_X} \mathcal{Q}\), as an abelian-group
808	  presheaf, as the coequalizer of the parallel pair, naturally in \(U\).
809	\end{proof}

844	\begin{lemma}[Sheafification inverts the whiskered localization unit]
845	  \label{lem:isIso_sheafification_whiskerRight_unit}
846	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.isIso_sheafification_whiskerRight_unit}
847	  \uses{lem:presheafModule_monoidal_mathlib,
848	    lem:presheafModule_sheafification_mathlib,
849	    lem:isIso_sheafification_map_iff, lem:localIso_toPresheaf_map_unit,
850	    lem:relativeTensor_as_coequalizer,
851	    lem:relativeTensor_objectwise_coequalizer}
852	  Let \(\mathcal{P}, \mathcal{Q}\) be presheaves of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules,
853	  let \(\eta_{\mathcal{P}} : \mathcal{P} \to (\mathcal{P})^{\#}\) be the unit of
854	  the sheafification adjunction
855	  (\cref{lem:presheafModule_sheafification_mathlib}), viewed as a morphism of
856	  presheaves of modules, and write
857	  \[
858	    \eta_{\mathcal{P}} \rhd \mathcal{Q} :
859	    \mathcal{P} \otimes_p \mathcal{Q}
860	    \longrightarrow (\mathcal{P})^{\#} \otimes_p \mathcal{Q}
861	  \]
862	  for its right whiskering by \(\mathcal{Q}\) in the presheaf monoidal structure
863	  (\cref{lem:presheafModule_monoidal_mathlib}). Then the image of
864	  \(\eta_{\mathcal{P}} \rhd \mathcal{Q}\) under the sheafification functor,
865	  \[
866	    (\eta_{\mathcal{P}} \rhd \mathcal{Q})^{\#} :
867	    (\mathcal{P} \otimes_p \mathcal{Q})^{\#}
868	    \xrightarrow{\ \sim\ }
869	    \bigl((\mathcal{P})^{\#} \otimes_p \mathcal{Q}\bigr)^{\#},
870	  \]
871	  is an isomorphism of sheaves of modules. This is the strong-monoidality
872	  comparison of the module sheafification functor on a whiskered unit.
873	\end{lemma}
874	
875	\begin{proof}
876	  \uses{lem:presheafModule_monoidal_mathlib,
877	    lem:presheafModule_sheafification_mathlib,
878	    lem:isIso_sheafification_map_iff, lem:localIso_toPresheaf_map_unit,
879	    lem:relativeTensor_as_coequalizer,
880	    lem:relativeTensor_objectwise_coequalizer,
881	    lem:snap_ztensor_whisker_localIso, def:relTensorDomainPresheaf}
882	  Module sheafification is a localization functor: it inverts exactly the class
883	  \(W\) of morphisms of presheaves of modules whose underlying morphism of
884	  abelian-group presheaves is a local isomorphism --- that is, lies in the class
885	  \(J.W\) of weak equivalences attached to the Grothendieck topology \(J\) on
886	  \(X\) (the inverse image of \(J.W\) along the forgetful functor to
887	  abelian-group presheaves). By the localization criterion for a reflective
888	  adjunction, \((f)^{\#}\) is an isomorphism if and only if \(f \in W\), i.e.\
889	  if and only if the underlying abelian-presheaf morphism of \(f\) lies in
890	  \(J.W\). It therefore suffices to prove that the underlying abelian morphism
891	  of \(\eta_{\mathcal{P}} \rhd \mathcal{Q}\) lies in \(J.W\).
892	
893	  \textbf{The relative tensor as a coequalizer.} The underlying abelian-group
894	  presheaf of the relative tensor product \(\mathcal{P} \otimes_p \mathcal{Q}\)
895	  is the objectwise relative tensor
896	  \(U \mapsto \mathcal{P}(U) \otimes_{\mathcal{O}_X(U)} \mathcal{Q}(U)\). For each
897	  \(U\) this module is the coequalizer of the two \(\mathcal{O}_X(U)\)-action
898	  maps
899	  \[
900	    \mathcal{P}(U) \otimes_{\mathbb{Z}} \mathcal{O}_X(U)
901	      \otimes_{\mathbb{Z}} \mathcal{Q}(U)
902	    \;\rightrightarrows\;
903	    \mathcal{P}(U) \otimes_{\mathbb{Z}} \mathcal{Q}(U),
904	  \]
905	  one acting on the left and one on the right factor. Colimits of abelian-group
906	  presheaves are computed objectwise, so \(\mathcal{P} \otimes_p \mathcal{Q}\),
907	  as an abelian-group presheaf, is the coequalizer of the corresponding parallel
908	  pair
909	  \[
910	    \mathcal{P} \otimes_{\mathbb{Z}} \mathcal{O}_X
911	      \otimes_{\mathbb{Z}} \mathcal{Q}
912	    \;\rightrightarrows\;
913	    \mathcal{P} \otimes_{\mathbb{Z}} \mathcal{Q}
914	  \]
915	  of \(\mathbb{Z}\)-tensor presheaves. Under this presentation the underlying
916	  abelian morphism of \(\eta_{\mathcal{P}} \rhd \mathcal{Q}\) is the map of
917	  coequalizers induced by whiskering the two rows with
918	  \(\eta_{\mathcal{P}} \otimes_{\mathbb{Z}} (-)\).
919	
920	  \textbf{Abelian sheafification preserves the coequalizer.} The abelian
921	  sheafification functor \(a\) (the associated-sheaf functor for \(J\) on
922	  abelian-group presheaves) is a left adjoint, hence preserves coequalizers; it
923	  therefore carries the parallel pair and its induced map to the analogous data
924	  of \(a\)-images. It remains to see that the two whiskered comparison maps
925	  relating the rows,
926	  \(\eta_{\mathcal{P}} \otimes_{\mathbb{Z}} \mathrm{id}\) and
927	  \(\eta_{\mathcal{P}} \otimes_{\mathbb{Z}} \mathrm{id}
